unlicensed_note: Licensed for use in children for topical application to minor wounds and skin infections

formulations.indian_conc_note: Neomycin 3400 IU + Polymyxin B 5000 IU + Bacitracin Zinc 250 IU per g
Neomycin 3400 IU + Polymyxin B 5000 IU + Bacitracin Zinc 250 IU per g

dosing_bands.indication: Superficial skin infections / Minor wound prophylaxis
dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply thin layer to affected area 2-3 times daily for up to 7 days
dosing_bands.notes: Do not apply to large open wounds or burn areas due to neomycin absorption risk; not for ophthalmic use

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to neomycin, polymyxin B, or bacitracin
Deep puncture wounds or serious burns (systemic absorption risk)
Use near eyes or on mucous membranes

interactions.effect: Additive nephrotoxicity and ototoxicity if applied over large areas with concurrent systemic aminoglycosides

monitoring_parameters: Signs of contact dermatitis or allergic sensitization
If used on large area: renal function, hearing assessment
pediatric_specific_warnings: Avoid application on large body surface area in neonates and infants — risk of systemic absorption
Neomycin sensitization can occur — discontinue if rash develops
food_instructions: Not applicable (topical use)
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; do not freeze
lactation_note: Topical use on small areas considered safe during lactation; avoid application on breast area
snomed_display: Neomycin + polymyxin B + bacitracin topical product

unlicensed_note: Licensed for use in children for myasthenia gravis and reversal of neuromuscular blockade

dosing_bands.duration_note: Single dose IV with atropine 0.02 mg/kg or glycopyrrolate 0.01 mg/kg
dosing_bands.notes: Always give with anticholinergic agent to prevent bradycardia; give slowly IV over 1 minute

dosing_bands.duration_note: Single dose IV with atropine 0.02 mg/kg or glycopyrrolate 0.01 mg/kg
dosing_bands.notes: Must be given with anticholinergic; ensure adequate spontaneous recovery before reversal

dosing_bands.duration_note: Oral: 0.3-1 mg/kg per dose every 4-6 hours; titrate to response
dosing_bands.notes: Titrate dose carefully to clinical response; risk of cholinergic crisis with overdose

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Reduce dose by 50% and titrate to response

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to neostigmine or bromides

interactions.effect: Neostigmine prolongs Phase I block of succinylcholine; do not use together
Aminoglycosides may antagonize effect of neostigmine at neuromuscular junction
Increased risk of bradycardia when combined

monitoring_parameters: Signs of cholinergic excess (salivation, lacrimation, urination, defecation)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Neonatal myasthenia: transient condition — assess before long-term therapy
Cholinergic crisis may mimic myasthenic crisis — differentiate before dose adjustment
Always have atropine available when administering IV neostigmine

administration.dilution: May dilute in 0.9% NaCl for slow IV injection
administration.infusion_rate_note: Give IV over 1 minute; must be accompanied by anticholinergic

food_instructions: May be taken with food to reduce GI side effects
storage_instructions: Store at 15-25°C; protect injection from light
lactation_note: Limited data; use with caution

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for children; used off-label in adolescent gynaecological conditions under specialist supervision

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5 mg TDS for acute bleed, then taper to 5 mg OD for 21 days; withdrawal bleed expected 2-4 days after stopping
dosing_bands.notes: Specialist supervision required in adolescents; rule out pregnancy before use

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 3 days before expected period; 5 mg TDS for up to 14 days; bleeding 2-3 days after stopping
dosing_bands.notes: Short-term use only; not a contraceptive at this dosing schedule

hepatic_note: Contraindicated in active liver disease or history of cholestatic jaundice with prior progestogen use
contraindications: Active liver disease or liver tumours

interactions.effect: Reduced efficacy of norethisterone due to increased hepatic metabolism
Progestogens may increase ciclosporin levels

monitoring_parameters: Liver function before and during treatment
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not recommended before menarche
Use in adolescents only under gynaecological or endocrine specialist supervision
May affect bone mineral density with prolonged use
food_instructions: Take with food to reduce nausea
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light and moisture
lactation_note: Small amounts excreted in breast milk; not recommended during lactation

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for children due to risk of cartilage damage; used off-label in severe UTI when no alternative available, under specialist guidance

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5-10 mg/kg/day in 2 divided doses for 7-14 days; only when culture shows resistance to safer alternatives
dosing_bands.notes: RESTRICTED IN PEDIATRICS: Risk of articular cartilage damage. Use only when benefit clearly outweighs risk and no alternative exists. Document justification.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-2 drops in affected eye(s) every 6 hours for 7 days; may increase to hourly for severe infection initially
dosing_bands.notes: Ophthalmic use licensed in children >=1 year; minimal systemic absorption

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Reduce to 400 mg once daily if CrCl <30 mL/min

contraindications: History of tendon disorders with fluoroquinolone use

interactions.effect: Markedly reduce norfloxacin absorption; give norfloxacin 2 hours before or 6 hours after
Norfloxacin may increase theophylline levels
Enhanced anticoagulant effect; monitor INR

monitoring_parameters: Urine culture before and after treatment
black_box_warnings: Risk of tendinitis and tendon rupture, especially in combination with corticosteroids
Risk of peripheral neuropathy — discontinue if symptoms occur
May exacerbate muscle weakness in myasthenia gravis
pediatric_specific_warnings: AVOID in children unless absolutely no alternative: risk of articular cartilage damage in weight-bearing joints
Document clinical justification for any pediatric systemic use
Ophthalmic use is generally safer with minimal systemic absorption
food_instructions: Take on empty stomach, 1 hour before or 2 hours after meals; take with a full glass of water
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light and moisture
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk; not recommended during lactation

therapeutic_use: National Immunization Schedule (NIS) — birth dose + boosters
unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages from birth; part of NIS India

formulations.indian_conc_note: Bivalent OPV (types 1 & 3); 2 drops per dose; 10-dose or 20-dose vial

dosing_bands.duration_note: Birth dose (OPV-0): 2 drops oral; NIS schedule: OPV-0 at birth, then OPV 1,2,3 at 6,10,14 weeks with IPV co-administration, booster OPV at 16-24 months and 5-6 years
dosing_bands.notes: Administer directly into mouth; if child spits out dose, repeat immediately. Breastfeeding need not be withheld. Keep cold chain 2-8°C.

contraindications: Anaphylaxis to previous dose or vaccine components

interactions.effect: May render vaccine ineffective and risk of vaccine-derived poliovirus infection in immunocompromised

monitoring_parameters: Monitor for vaccine-associated paralytic poliomyelitis (VAPP) — extremely rare
pediatric_specific_warnings: VAPP risk approximately 1 in 2.7 million first doses — benefits vastly outweigh risks
IPV should be given concomitantly per NIS to mitigate VAPP risk
Avoid in known immunodeficiency — use IPV only

administration.compatibility_note: Can be given simultaneously with other childhood vaccines at different sites
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C; protect from light; use within 28 days of opening multi-dose vial

food_instructions: No food restrictions; breastfeeding need not be withheld before or after vaccine
storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C (cold chain); do not freeze; protect from light
lactation_note: Safe to administer to infants of breastfeeding mothers
reference_source: WHO Position Paper on Polio Vaccines

therapeutic_use: Prevention of dehydration in acute gastroenteritis
unlicensed_note: Fully licensed for all ages including neonates; WHO-recommended formulation

formulations.indian_conc_note: WHO-ORS: NaCl 2.6g, KCl 1.5g, Na citrate 2.9g, Glucose anhydrous 13.5g per litre; osmolarity 245 mOsm/L

dosing_bands.duration_note: 50-100 mL ORS after each loose stool; continue breastfeeding; maintain until diarrhoea stops
dosing_bands.notes: Give small frequent sips with spoon or cup — not bottle. Continue breastfeeding. Add zinc supplementation.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 100-200 mL ORS after each loose stool; encourage ad libitum drinking between episodes
dosing_bands.notes: WHO Plan A for prevention/treatment of mild dehydration. Always combine with zinc supplementation for 14 days.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 75 mL/kg over 4 hours in ORT corner; reassess hydration status after 4 hours
dosing_bands.notes: Administer under supervision in ORT/observation area. If vomiting, give small sips every 1-2 minutes. Reassess at 4 hours. If worsening, switch to IV fluids.

contraindications: Intractable vomiting (relative — try small sips first)
monitoring_parameters: Hydration status (skin turgor, mucous membranes, urine output)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Do NOT add sugar, salt, or other additives to prepared ORS
Discard prepared solution after 24 hours at room temperature
Use cup and spoon, not bottle, in infants to prevent aspiration
Always supplement with zinc (10 mg <6 months, 20 mg >6 months for 14 days)

administration.reconstitution: Dissolve entire sachet in 1 litre of clean drinking water; do not boil after preparation
administration.storage: Prepared solution: use within 24 hours at room temperature; discard remaining

food_instructions: Give between meals; continue normal feeding and breastfeeding during rehydration
storage_instructions: Dry sachets: store below 30°C in dry place; prepared solution: use within 24 hours
lactation_note: Safe to use during lactation; encourage continued breastfeeding
reference_source: IAP Guidelines on Acute Diarrhoeal Disease

therapeutic_use: Prevention and treatment of postpartum haemorrhage
unlicensed_note: Not indicated in pediatric practice; included in formulary for maternal emergencies managed in hospital and for neonatal resuscitation support context

dosing_bands.duration_note: 10 IU IM after delivery of anterior shoulder (AMTSL); alternative: 5 IU slow IV
dosing_bands.notes: Hospital/maternal use only; included for emergency obstetric care. Not a pediatric drug.

interactions.effect: Synergistic uterotonic effect; risk of uterine hyperstimulation and rupture
Increased risk of hypertension when combined

black_box_warnings: Not for elective induction of labour for convenience
Risk of uterine rupture especially in multiparous women and prior uterine surgery
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not a pediatric drug; included for maternal emergency protocols only

administration.infusion_rate_note: 10 IU IM single dose for PPH prevention

administration.dilution: Dilute in 0.9% NaCl or Ringer's lactate for infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: For induction: start at 1-2 mU/min, increase by 1-2 mU every 30 min; max 20 mU/min

food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable)
storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C; protect from light; do not freeze
lactation_note: Negligible amount in breast milk; enhances milk let-down

unlicensed_note: IV formulation licensed for children >=2 years; oral formulation has limited pediatric data but widely used off-label in neonates/infants under specialist guidance

formulations.indian_conc_note: Pantoprazole sodium 40 mg lyophilized powder for reconstitution

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 mg/kg/day once daily; review at 4 weeks; specialist use only in infants
dosing_bands.notes: Off-label in infants <2 years; most infant reflux is physiological and does not require acid suppression. Use only if proven acid-related pathology.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5-1 mg/kg/day once daily before breakfast; 15-40 kg: 20 mg OD; >40 kg: 40 mg OD; review at 4-8 weeks
dosing_bands.notes: Do not crush enteric-coated tablets; use granule sachets for children who cannot swallow tablets

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 mg/kg IV once daily (max 40 mg); switch to oral when tolerating feeds
dosing_bands.notes: IV use in critically ill children; reconstitute with 10 mL NS, give over 2-15 minutes

hepatic_note: In severe hepatic impairment: max dose 20 mg/day; monitor LFTs
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to pantoprazole or substituted benzimidazoles
cross_reactions: Other PPIs (omeprazole, esomeprazole, rabeprazole) — rare cross-reactivity

interactions.effect: PPI reduces absorption of these antiretrovirals — contraindicated
PPI may increase methotrexate levels; consider temporary discontinuation with high-dose MTX
Long-term acid suppression may reduce absorption of iron, calcium, and vitamin B12
Pantoprazole has less CYP2C19 interaction than omeprazole but monitor if combined

monitoring_parameters: Consider C. difficile if diarrhoea develops
pediatric_specific_warnings: Do not use PPI for physiological GER in infants — no evidence of benefit
Long-term PPI use in children associated with increased respiratory and GI infections
Step-down to lowest effective dose; plan for discontinuation

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute 40 mg vial with 10 mL 0.9% NaCl
administration.dilution: May further dilute in 100 mL 0.9% NaCl or D5W for infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Give IV bolus over 2-15 minutes; or infusion over 15-30 minutes
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with 0.9% NaCl, D5W; incompatible with Ringer's lactate
administration.storage: Use reconstituted solution within 12 hours at room temperature

food_instructions: Take 30 minutes before breakfast on empty stomach; swallow whole, do not crush or chew
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from moisture; keep in original blister pack
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk; weigh benefit vs risk

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=1 year for moderate to severe pain; use with extreme caution and specialist oversight

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5-1 mg/kg per dose IM/SC/IV every 3-4 hours as needed; use lowest effective dose for shortest duration
dosing_bands.notes: Use under specialist supervision in children; monitor respiratory rate and sedation level; have naloxone available

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Reduce dose by 50-75%; active metabolites may accumulate

hepatic_note: Reduce dose in hepatic impairment; avoid in severe hepatic disease
cross_reactions: Other opioids — may precipitate withdrawal in opioid-dependent patients due to antagonist properties

interactions.effect: Enhanced respiratory depression and sedation; reduce doses of both
Pentazocine may antagonize pure agonist analgesia and precipitate withdrawal
Risk of serotonin syndrome and enhanced CNS effects; avoid combination

monitoring_parameters: Respiratory rate (hold if <12/min in children, <20/min in infants)
black_box_warnings: Risk of addiction, abuse, and misuse — use lowest effective dose for shortest duration
Respiratory depression risk — monitor closely in children
Schedule H (India) controlled substance
pediatric_specific_warnings: Higher incidence of dysphoric/psychotomimetic effects compared to other opioids
Not first-line analgesic in pediatrics — prefer paracetamol, ibuprofen, or morphine
Avoid in children <1 year due to limited safety data

administration.infusion_rate_note: IM preferred over SC to reduce tissue irritation

administration.dilution: May dilute in 0.9% NaCl for slow IV injection
administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with barbiturates in same syringe — precipitation

food_instructions: Oral: may take with food to reduce GI upset
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light; Schedule H controlled substance — locked storage
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk; may cause neonatal respiratory depression; avoid

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=1 year; widely used in Indian pediatric practice

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5 mg/kg/day in 2-3 divided doses; use with caution in infants due to sedation risk
dosing_bands.notes: Prefer second-generation antihistamines (cetirizine) in infants; first-gen antihistamines carry sedation and paradoxical excitation risk

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5-1 mg/kg/day in 2-3 divided doses; 1-5 years: 7.5-15 mg/day; 6-12 years: 15-30 mg/day
dosing_bands.notes: Second-generation antihistamines preferred for routine allergic conditions; use first-gen when sedation is beneficial (e.g., pruritus at night)

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5-1 mg/kg slow IV/IM as single dose; for anaphylaxis: ALWAYS give adrenaline first — antihistamine is adjunctive only
dosing_bands.notes: In anaphylaxis: adrenaline IM is first-line, NOT antihistamines. Pheniramine is adjunctive for symptom relief.

hepatic_note: Use with caution in hepatic impairment; may worsen hepatic encephalopathy
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to pheniramine or antihistamines

interactions.effect: Enhanced sedation and respiratory depression
Additive anticholinergic effects — urinary retention, dry mouth, tachycardia
Prolonged and intensified anticholinergic and sedative effects

pediatric_specific_warnings: Risk of paradoxical CNS stimulation (excitation, insomnia) in young children
Avoid in neonates — risk of seizures and apnoea
Second-generation antihistamines (cetirizine, levocetirizine) are preferred first-line in pediatrics

administration.dilution: Dilute in 10-20 mL 0.9% NaCl for slow IV injection
administration.infusion_rate_note: Give slowly IV over 3-5 minutes to avoid hypotension

food_instructions: May be taken with or without food; take with food if GI upset occurs
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light and moisture
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk; may cause drowsiness in infant; use with caution

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for children <12 years in most countries; used off-label for juvenile idiopathic arthritis under specialist guidance. Licensed for adolescents >=16 years for short-term use.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.2-0.4 mg/kg/day once daily; max 15 mg/day for <46 kg; max 20 mg/day for >=46 kg; long-term specialist use only
dosing_bands.notes: NOT first-line NSAID for JIA — prefer ibuprofen or naproxen. Use only if other NSAIDs failed. Long half-life allows once daily dosing.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 20 mg once daily for short-term use (max 14 days); reduce to 10 mg/day for maintenance if needed
dosing_bands.notes: Short-term use preferred; higher GI toxicity compared to ibuprofen/naproxen

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Avoid in severe renal impairment; if essential, use lowest dose with close monitoring

hepatic_note: Reduce dose in hepatic impairment; avoid in severe liver disease
contraindications: History of GI bleeding with NSAID use

interactions.effect: Increased risk of GI bleeding; piroxicam has high protein binding displacement
Reduced renal clearance of MTX; risk of toxicity
Reduced antihypertensive effect; increased nephrotoxicity risk
NSAIDs may increase lithium levels

black_box_warnings: Increased risk of serious cardiovascular thrombotic events including MI and stroke
Increased risk of serious GI adverse events including bleeding, ulceration, and perforation
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not first-line NSAID in children — prefer ibuprofen or naproxen
Long half-life (50 hours) means drug accumulation risk
Higher GI toxicity profile compared to other NSAIDs
food_instructions: Take with food or milk to reduce GI irritation
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from moisture
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk in small amounts; avoid if possible

unlicensed_note: Licensed from 6 weeks of age; part of NIS India (Haryana: PCV free under UIP) and IAP schedule

formulations.indian_conc_note: 13 pneumococcal serotypes conjugated to CRM197; 0.5 mL pre-filled syringe
10 pneumococcal serotypes conjugated to CRM197; 0.5 mL; used in NIS India

dosing_bands.duration_note: NIS: PCV-1 at 6 weeks, PCV-2 at 14 weeks, PCV-Booster at 9-12 months. IAP: PCV-1 at 6 weeks, PCV-2 at 10 weeks, PCV-3 at 14 weeks, Booster at 15-18 months.
dosing_bands.notes: IM injection in anterolateral thigh (infants) or deltoid (older children). Haryana provides PCV free under UIP. Can be given with other routine vaccines.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2 doses, 8 weeks apart; for PCV13 — if unvaccinated child 12-23 months
dosing_bands.notes: Catch-up schedule depends on age at first dose and product used; refer to IAP guidelines

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1 dose of PCV13 for healthy children >=2 years; high-risk children may need PCV13 + PPSV23 sequence
dosing_bands.notes: High-risk: sickle cell, asplenia, HIV, cochlear implant, immunocompromised; follow IAP/ACIP guidance for PCV+PPSV schedule

contraindications: Anaphylaxis to previous dose or any vaccine component (including diphtheria toxoid for CRM197-conjugated vaccines)

interactions.effect: May reduce immune response to vaccine; vaccinate at least 2 weeks before starting immunosuppression if possible

monitoring_parameters: Local reactions (redness, swelling at injection site)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Apnoea risk in premature infants (<28 weeks GA) — monitor for 48-72 hours after vaccination
Fever and irritability common but self-limiting; manage with paracetamol if needed
Not a substitute for PPSV23 in high-risk children >=2 years who need both

administration.infusion_rate_note: Shake well before use; 0.5 mL IM in anterolateral thigh (infants) or deltoid (>1 year)
administration.compatibility_note: Can be given simultaneously with other childhood vaccines at different sites; do not mix in same syringe
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C; do not freeze; discard if frozen

food_instructions: Not applicable (injectable vaccine)
storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C (cold chain); do not freeze — freezing destroys vaccine; protect from light
lactation_note: Safe to administer to infants of breastfeeding mothers

unlicensed_note: Licensed for external use in children; avoid in neonates <2 months and premature infants due to thyroid absorption risk

formulations.indian_conc_note: Povidone iodine 5% w/v solution (0.5% available iodine)
Povidone iodine 10% w/v solution (1% available iodine)

dosing_bands.duration_note: Apply 5-10% solution to affected area 1-2 times daily; allow to dry before dressing; use 5% for sensitive areas
dosing_bands.notes: Avoid use on large open wounds in infants — risk of iodine absorption and thyroid suppression. Let dry for 2 minutes for full bactericidal effect.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Dilute 10-15 mL of 2% gargle in equal amount of warm water; gargle for 30 seconds, spit out; do not swallow; 3-4 times daily
dosing_bands.notes: Only for children old enough to gargle and spit (usually >=6 years); do not swallow

contraindications: Thyroid disorders (relative — short-term topical use OK)
cross_reactions: Iodine-containing contrast media (not true cross-reactivity but monitor)

interactions.effect: Do not use simultaneously — risk of caustic injury
Chronic large-area application may cause additive thyroid suppression with lithium

monitoring_parameters: Thyroid function if used on large areas in infants/neonates
pediatric_specific_warnings: AVOID in neonates <2 months and premature infants — systemic iodine absorption can cause transient hypothyroidism
Limit area and duration of application in infants 2-12 months
Do not use under occlusive dressings on large areas in young children

administration.dilution: May dilute 10% solution to 5% for sensitive areas or young children
administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with mercury-based antiseptics; inactivated by blood and pus (re-apply if contaminated)

food_instructions: Not applicable (topical/gargle use); gargle — do not swallow
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light; keep container tightly closed
lactation_note: Short-term topical use on small areas is safe during lactation; avoid on breast area

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for children; combination product used in adults for neuropathic pain. Pediatric use extremely limited and only under specialist neurology guidance.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 75 mg BID, may increase to 150 mg BID after 1 week based on response; specialist supervision only
dosing_bands.notes: Pregabalin component: Schedule H in India. Very limited pediatric data. Use individual components if dose titration needed in younger children.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Reduce pregabalin dose to 50-75% of normal; use individual components for precise adjustment
Reduce pregabalin dose to 25-50% of normal; extend interval; use individual components

hepatic_note: Pregabalin not hepatically metabolized; no adjustment needed
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to pregabalin or methylcobalamin
Severe renal impairment without dose adjustment

interactions.effect: Enhanced sedation and respiratory depression risk; pregabalin potentiates CNS depression
Increased risk of angioedema when pregabalin combined with ACE inhibitors
Additive peripheral oedema and weight gain

monitoring_parameters: Suicidal ideation (rare but reported with pregabalin)
black_box_warnings: Pregabalin: risk of suicidal thoughts and behaviour — monitor closely in adolescents
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not recommended for children <12 years — insufficient safety and efficacy data
Risk of sedation, dizziness, weight gain in adolescents
Abrupt withdrawal may cause seizures — taper over at least 1 week
Potential for misuse/abuse — Schedule H substance
food_instructions: May be taken with or without food
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from moisture and light
lactation_note: Pregabalin excreted in breast milk; not recommended during lactation

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for pediatric use; used off-label by pediatric endocrinologists for precocious puberty management and in adolescent gynaecology

formulations.indian_conc_note: Micronized progesterone 100 mg soft gelatin capsule
Micronized progesterone 200 mg soft gelatin capsule
Progesterone 50 mg/mL in oil; 1 mL ampoule

dosing_bands.duration_note: 200-300 mg at bedtime for 12 days in second half of cycle; specialist supervision required
dosing_bands.notes: Specialist use only in adolescents; rule out pregnancy before use; take at bedtime to minimize drowsiness

hepatic_note: Contraindicated in severe hepatic impairment; use with caution in mild-moderate hepatic disease
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to progesterone or peanut/soya (capsule excipients)

interactions.effect: Increased metabolism of progesterone; reduced efficacy
Increased progesterone levels; monitor for enhanced effects

pediatric_specific_warnings: Not for use before menarche
Specialist endocrine supervision mandatory in adolescents
Drowsiness is common — advise taking at bedtime
food_instructions: Take at bedtime with water; food may delay absorption but taking at bedtime minimizes drowsiness
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from moisture and light
lactation_note: Naturally occurring hormone; small amounts in breast milk; considered safe

therapeutic_use: Induction and maintenance of general anaesthesia
unlicensed_note: Licensed for induction of anaesthesia in children >=1 month and maintenance in children >=3 years. ICU sedation NOT licensed in children — associated with propofol infusion syndrome.

formulations.indian_conc_note: Propofol 1% (10 mg/mL) lipid emulsion; 20 mL ampoule
Propofol 2% (20 mg/mL) lipid emulsion; 50 mL vial

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2.5-4 mg/kg IV; titrate slowly in 1 mg/kg aliquots every 20-30 seconds until loss of consciousness
dosing_bands.notes: Infants 1-6 months may require higher induction doses (up to 4 mg/kg). Painful on injection — may give lidocaine 1 mg/kg IV before or mix with propofol.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2-3.5 mg/kg IV; titrate in aliquots every 20-30 seconds; young children may need higher end of range
dosing_bands.notes: Higher doses needed in younger children (3-8 years). Reduce dose in ASA III/IV patients. Always have airway equipment ready.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 9-15 mg/kg/hr continuous infusion; titrate to clinical effect; reduce rate as anaesthesia deepens
dosing_bands.notes: Licensed for TIVA maintenance in children >=3 years. Always combine with analgesic (fentanyl/remifentanil). Monitor depth with clinical signs.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-2 mg/kg IV initial bolus; titrate 0.5 mg/kg aliquots every 2-3 minutes as needed; max total dose usually 4-5 mg/kg
dosing_bands.notes: Only by trained personnel with airway management skills. Fasting guidelines apply. Must have full monitoring and resuscitation equipment.

hepatic_note: Reduce dose in severe hepatic impairment; slower metabolism
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to propofol, soya, or egg lecithin
ICU sedation in children (propofol infusion syndrome risk)
cross_reactions: Egg allergy (contains egg lecithin — true anaphylaxis rare but caution)

interactions.effect: Synergistic respiratory and cardiovascular depression; reduce propofol dose when combined
Enhanced sedation and respiratory depression; reduce propofol dose
Propofol does not prevent suxamethonium-induced fasciculations or bradycardia

monitoring_parameters: Triglyceride levels (if prolonged use >3 days — rarely indicated in pediatrics)
Signs of propofol infusion syndrome (metabolic acidosis, rhabdomyolysis, hyperkalaemia)
black_box_warnings: PROPOFOL INFUSION SYNDROME: Potentially fatal — metabolic acidosis, rhabdomyolysis, cardiac failure, hyperkalaemia. Risk increases with >4 mg/kg/hr for >48 hours. AVOID prolonged ICU sedation in children.
Not for ICU sedation in children — use alternative agents
pediatric_specific_warnings: Propofol infusion syndrome is more common in children than adults — DO NOT use for ICU sedation
Higher induction doses needed in young children compared to adults
Injection pain is common — pretreat with lidocaine or use large vein
Apnoea is common after induction — be prepared to ventilate

administration.dilution: May dilute 1% with D5W only to minimum 2 mg/mL; shake well before use
administration.infusion_rate_note: Induction: give in aliquots every 20-30 seconds. Maintenance: continuous infusion via syringe pump.
administration.compatibility_note: May be diluted with D5W only; do not mix with other drugs in same syringe; co-administer lidocaine to reduce injection pain
administration.storage: Store below 25°C; do not freeze; shake before use; discard 12 hours after opening (no preservative)

food_instructions: Not applicable (IV anaesthetic); standard fasting guidelines: 6h solids, 4h breast milk, 2h clear fluids pre-anaesthesia
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; do not freeze; do not refrigerate (may separate); use within 12 hours of opening vial
lactation_note: Rapidly cleared; short exposure during anaesthesia compatible with breastfeeding after recovery

unlicensed_note: Not licensed for children; used off-label for pediatric hypertension and proteinuric renal disease under pediatric nephrology/cardiology supervision

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start 0.05 mg/kg/day once daily; titrate up every 2 weeks to max 0.1-0.2 mg/kg/day based on BP response
dosing_bands.notes: Off-label pediatric use; monitor BP carefully after first dose (risk of first-dose hypotension). Check K+ and creatinine at baseline and 1-2 weeks after initiation.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Start low, titrate to target proteinuria reduction; long-term use under nephrology supervision
dosing_bands.notes: Used for antiproteinuric effect in IgA nephropathy, FSGS, diabetic nephropathy. Monitor urine protein:creatinine ratio.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Start at 25% of normal dose; titrate cautiously with close monitoring of K+ and creatinine

hepatic_note: Reduce initial dose; ramipril is a prodrug (activated by hepatic esterases); impaired activation in liver disease
contraindications: History of angioedema with ACE inhibitor

interactions.effect: Risk of life-threatening hyperkalaemia; avoid combination or monitor K+ closely
Reduced antihypertensive effect; increased nephrotoxicity risk
ACE inhibitors increase lithium levels; monitor closely
Additive hyperkalaemic effect; monitor K+

monitoring_parameters: Serum potassium at baseline, 1-2 weeks, then periodically
Serum creatinine / eGFR at baseline and after each dose change
black_box_warnings: TERATOGENIC: ACE inhibitors cause fetal renal agenesis, oligohydramnios, and death when used in pregnancy. Discontinue immediately if pregnancy detected.
pediatric_specific_warnings: First-dose hypotension risk — give first dose at bedtime or under observation
ABSOLUTELY contraindicated in adolescent females of childbearing age without reliable contraception
Monitor growth and renal function in long-term pediatric use
Dry cough is common — if intolerable, switch to ARB (losartan)

administration.compatibility_note: Capsule contents can be mixed with water or apple juice for children unable to swallow capsules

food_instructions: May be taken with or without food; take at same time daily
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from moisture; keep in original blister pack
lactation_note: Excreted in breast milk; not recommended during lactation
reference_source: AAP Clinical Practice Guideline for Pediatric Hypertension

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=6 years with heterozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia; >=10 years for some formulations

dosing_bands.duration_note: 6-9 years: start 5 mg OD, max 10 mg OD; 10-17 years: start 5 mg OD, may increase to 10-20 mg OD after 4 weeks
dosing_bands.notes: Specialist lipidology/cardiology supervision required. Dietary modification for 6-12 months before considering statin. Monitor LFTs and CK at baseline and periodically.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 20 mg once daily (children >=10 years); adjunct to other lipid-lowering therapies; specialist use only
dosing_bands.notes: Usually combined with ezetimibe and/or LDL apheresis; specialist centre management

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Start at 5 mg; max 10 mg; doses >10 mg contraindicated in severe renal impairment

hepatic_note: Contraindicated in active liver disease or unexplained persistent transaminase elevation

interactions.effect: Markedly increases rosuvastatin levels — CONTRAINDICATED
Rosuvastatin may increase INR; monitor closely
Increased risk of myopathy/rhabdomyolysis; avoid combination
Aluminium/magnesium antacids reduce rosuvastatin absorption; give rosuvastatin 2 hours after antacid

monitoring_parameters: Fasting lipid profile at baseline, 4-6 weeks, then every 3-6 months
LFTs (ALT) at baseline and as clinically indicated
Growth and pubertal development in children on long-term therapy
pediatric_specific_warnings: Not recommended for children <6 years
Must be combined with dietary intervention and lifestyle modification
Contraindicated in adolescent females who may become pregnant
Monitor growth and pubertal development annually
food_instructions: May be taken with or without food; take at same time daily (evening preferred)
storage_instructions: Store below 30°C; protect from moisture and light
lactation_note: Contraindicated during lactation; cholesterol is essential for infant development

therapeutic_use: Part of NIS India and IAP immunization schedule
unlicensed_note: Licensed for infants from 6 weeks of age; part of NIS India (Haryana: Rotavirus vaccine free under UIP)

formulations.indian_conc_note: Live attenuated bovine-human reassortant rotavirus 116E strain; 0.5 mL per dose; 5-dose vial
Live bovine-human reassortant pentavalent rotavirus vaccine; lyophilized; reconstitute with 2.5 mL diluent

dosing_bands.duration_note: NIS (Rotavac): 3 doses at 6, 10, 14 weeks. IAP (Rotasiil/Rotavac): 3 doses at 6, 10, 14 weeks. Do not start after 15 weeks of age. Complete series by 8 months.
dosing_bands.notes: Oral administration only. Haryana provides rotavirus vaccine free under UIP. If child spits out or vomits dose, do NOT repeat. Breastfeeding does not need to be withheld.

contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to previous dose or vaccine components
Uncorrected congenital malformation of GI tract (e.g., Meckel's diverticulum)
Age >8 months (do not initiate or continue series)

interactions.effect: May render vaccine ineffective; risk of vaccine strain gastroenteritis in immunocompromised

monitoring_parameters: Monitor for signs of intussusception for 7 days post-vaccination (irritability, blood in stool, abdominal distension, vomiting)
pediatric_specific_warnings: Small increased risk of intussusception (~1-6 per 100,000 vaccinated infants) — mainly within 7 days of first dose
Do NOT initiate series after 15 weeks of age; complete by 8 months
Shedding of vaccine virus in stool for up to 15 days — advise hand hygiene for caregivers
Pre-term infants can be vaccinated at chronological age per schedule

administration.reconstitution: Rotasiil: reconstitute lyophilized vaccine with 2.5 mL diluent provided
administration.compatibility_note: Can be given simultaneously with other oral and injectable vaccines at same visit
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C; do not freeze; Rotavac can be kept at room temperature for limited period (CTC)

food_instructions: No food restrictions; breastfeeding need not be withheld before or after vaccine
storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C (cold chain); do not freeze; use reconstituted vaccine within 4 hours
lactation_note: Safe to administer to breastfed infants
reference_source: WHO Position Paper on Rotavirus Vaccines

drug_class: Inhalational general anaesthetic (fluorinated methyl isopropyl ether)
therapeutic_use: Preferred inhalational agent for pediatric anaesthesia
unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages including neonates; preferred inhalational agent for pediatric induction due to non-irritant nature

formulations.indian_conc_note: Sevoflurane 100% liquid for use in calibrated vaporiser; 250 mL bottle

dosing_bands.duration_note: Vital capacity or tidal breathing induction: start at 6-8% in 100% O2 or O2/N2O; loss of consciousness in 1-2 minutes
dosing_bands.notes: Preferred agent for inhalational induction in infants and children — pleasant smell, non-irritant to airways. MAC in neonates ~3.3%, infants ~3.2%.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 6-8% in O2 or O2/N2O for induction; reduce to maintenance once IV access secured
dosing_bands.notes: MAC decreases with age: 1-6 months ~3.2%, 6m-3y ~2.8%, 3-12y ~2.5%. Children require higher MAC than adults.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-3% (0.5-1.0 MAC) with O2/air or O2/N2O; titrate to clinical effect and end-tidal concentration
dosing_bands.notes: Adjust based on surgical stimulus and concurrent analgesics. Monitor end-tidal sevoflurane concentration. Rapid emergence — plan analgesia accordingly.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Use with caution — fluoride metabolite may accumulate; keep fresh gas flows >2 L/min; avoid prolonged procedures

hepatic_note: Rare cases of hepatotoxicity reported; use with caution if prior halogenated anaesthetic hepatitis
contraindications: Known or suspected susceptibility to malignant hyperthermia
Known hypersensitivity to sevoflurane or other halogenated agents
History of confirmed hepatotoxicity to halogenated anaesthetics
cross_reactions: Other halogenated inhalational anaesthetics (isoflurane, desflurane, halothane)

interactions.effect: Sevoflurane potentiates succinylcholine; increased risk of malignant hyperthermia in susceptible individuals
Sevoflurane enhances neuromuscular blockade — may need reduced doses of relaxants
Enhanced hypotension and myocardial depression

black_box_warnings: Risk of malignant hyperthermia — have dantrolene available
FDA warning: repeated/prolonged exposure to general anaesthetics in children <3 years may affect brain development — use only when medically necessary
pediatric_specific_warnings: Emergence agitation/delirium common (30-80% in preschool children) — may need rescue with propofol, clonidine, or fentanyl
Higher MAC in younger children — need higher concentrations than adults
Bradycardia more common in children than adults — have atropine ready
Neurotoxicity concern with prolonged/repeated exposure <3 years — discuss risk-benefit with parents

administration.infusion_rate_note: Use only in calibrated sevoflurane vaporiser; fresh gas flows 2-6 L/min for induction, can reduce for maintenance
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with standard anaesthesia circuits; avoid desiccated CO2 absorbents (Compound A formation)
administration.storage: Store below 25°C; keep bottle tightly closed; no refrigeration needed

food_instructions: Pre-anaesthetic fasting: 6h solids, 4h breast milk, 2h clear fluids
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C in original bottle; keep tightly closed; no special storage requirements
lactation_note: Rapidly eliminated; short exposure during anaesthesia compatible with breastfeeding after full recovery

unlicensed_note: OTC formulation; widely used in Indian pediatric practice for infantile colic; limited evidence base but long safety record

formulations.indian_conc_note: Simethicone 40 mg + Dill oil 0.3 mL + Fennel oil 0.005 mL per mL

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.3 mL (6 drops) after each feed, up to 3-4 times daily; use after feeds
dosing_bands.notes: Symptomatic relief only; counsel parents about normal infant crying patterns. Infantile colic usually resolves by 3-4 months.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 0.5-1 mL after each feed, up to 3-4 times daily
dosing_bands.notes: Generally well tolerated; limited evidence for efficacy but excellent safety profile

pediatric_specific_warnings: Limited evidence for efficacy in infantile colic; parental reassurance and dietary review equally important
Rule out organic causes if persistent or severe symptoms
Contains herbal oils — rare possibility of allergic reaction

administration.dilution: May be mixed with small amount of cooled boiled water or given directly into mouth

food_instructions: Give after each feed; may mix with small amount of water
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; use within 3 months of opening
lactation_note: Given directly to infant; not a concern for breastfeeding

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages; standard maintenance IV fluid in pediatric practice; NOTE: isotonic fluids (N/2 + D5 or NS + D5) preferred over hypotonic fluids per current guidelines

formulations.indian_conc_note: NaCl 0.9% + Dextrose 5% (Na 154 mEq/L, Cl 154 mEq/L, Glucose 50 g/L)

formulations.form: IV infusion (0.45% NaCl + 5% Dextrose — N/2 in D5)
formulations.indian_conc_note: NaCl 0.45% + Dextrose 5% (Na 77 mEq/L); half-normal saline + dextrose

formulations.form: IV infusion (0.2% NaCl + 5% Dextrose — N/5 in D5)
formulations.indian_conc_note: NaCl 0.2% + Dextrose 5% (Na 34 mEq/L); one-fifth NS + dextrose — AVOID in children (hyponatraemia risk)

dosing_bands.duration_note: First 10 kg: 100 mL/kg/day (4 mL/kg/hr); use isotonic fluid (0.9% NaCl + D5 or 0.45% NaCl + D5 with 20 mEq/L KCl)
dosing_bands.notes: Current guidelines: use ISOTONIC maintenance fluids (0.9% NaCl + D5 or 0.45% NaCl + D5) to prevent hospital-acquired hyponatraemia. AVOID N/5 in D5.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Holliday-Segar: first 10 kg = 100 mL/kg/day, next 10 kg = 50 mL/kg/day, each subsequent kg = 20 mL/kg/day. Use 0.9% NaCl+D5 or 0.45% NaCl+D5 with 20 mEq/L KCl once voiding.
dosing_bands.notes: Add KCl (20 mEq/L) once urine output confirmed. Restrict to 50-80% maintenance for post-op, meningitis, SIADH. Switch to oral as soon as tolerated.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Initial bolus: 0.9% NaCl 20 mL/kg over 20-30 min (may repeat up to 3 times); then deficit replacement + maintenance with 0.9% NaCl + D5 over 24-48 hours
dosing_bands.notes: Bolus should be with 0.9% NaCl (NO dextrose). Dextrose-saline for maintenance phase only. Reassess after each bolus. Calculate deficit = %dehydration x weight(kg) x 10 mL.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Restrict fluid volume; avoid potassium supplementation; monitor fluid balance and electrolytes closely

hepatic_note: Use sodium-restricted fluids in ascites/hepatic oedema
contraindications: Hyperglycaemia (avoid dextrose-containing fluids until glucose controlled)

interactions.effect: Dextrose-containing fluids may require insulin adjustment in diabetic patients
Sodium-containing fluids may exacerbate fluid retention with steroids

monitoring_parameters: Serum electrolytes (Na, K, Cl) every 12-24 hours
Signs of fluid overload (oedema, tachycardia, respiratory distress)
pediatric_specific_warnings: AVOID hypotonic fluids (N/5 in D5) for routine maintenance — risk of fatal hospital-acquired hyponatraemia
Use isotonic fluids (0.9% NaCl+D5 or 0.45% NaCl+D5) per NICE/AAP guidelines
Neonates: risk of hyperglycaemia with D10; risk of hypoglycaemia without dextrose — monitor BGL
Always calculate maintenance rate accurately using Holliday-Segar formula

administration.infusion_rate_note: Bolus: 20 mL/kg over 20-30 min using 0.9% NaCl. Maintenance: continuous infusion via infusion pump using Holliday-Segar rate.
administration.compatibility_note: Check compatibility before adding medications; KCl commonly added (max 40 mEq/L peripheral, 60 mEq/L central)
administration.storage: Store at room temperature; do not freeze; inspect for particulate matter before use

food_instructions: Not applicable (IV use); switch to oral fluids/feeding as soon as tolerated
storage_instructions: Store at room temperature (15-30°C); do not freeze; discard unused portion
lactation_note: Safe; physiological fluids
reference_source: NICE IV Fluid Therapy in Children Guidelines
snomed_display: Sodium chloride and glucose infusion solution

unlicensed_note: Licensed for children >=4 years for constipation; younger children off-label with specialist guidance

formulations.indian_conc_note: Sodium picosulfate 7.5 mg/mL oral drops (15 drops = 1 mL = 7.5 mg)

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2-5 drops (1-2.5 mg) at bedtime; off-label use in infants <4 years; prefer osmotic laxatives (lactulose) first
dosing_bands.notes: Not first-line for infantile constipation — prefer lactulose or polyethylene glycol. Use only for short-term relief.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 4-10 years: 2.5-5 mg (5-10 drops) at bedtime; >10 years: 5-10 mg at bedtime; short-term use only
dosing_bands.notes: Give at bedtime; effect in 6-12 hours (morning bowel movement). Not for chronic daily use. Treat underlying cause of constipation.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 5-10 mg at bedtime for 2 days before procedure; combined with polyethylene glycol for optimal preparation
dosing_bands.notes: Part of bowel prep regimen; ensure adequate hydration

interactions.effect: Chronic stimulant laxative use may worsen electrolyte imbalance (hypokalaemia)
Antibiotics may reduce efficacy of sodium picosulfate (requires colonic bacteria for activation)

pediatric_specific_warnings: Not first-line for childhood constipation — prefer PEG 3350 or lactulose
Avoid chronic daily use — risk of laxative dependence
Ensure adequate fluid intake during treatment

administration.dilution: Drops may be mixed with water or juice

food_instructions: Give at bedtime; may take with or without food; ensure adequate water intake
storage_instructions: Store below 25°C; protect from light
lactation_note: Not absorbed systemically; safe during lactation

unlicensed_note: Not specifically licensed for children; used off-label in life-threatening thrombotic events under pediatric haematology/ICU specialist supervision

formulations.indian_conc_note: Streptokinase 750,000 IU lyophilized powder for reconstitution
Streptokinase 1,500,000 IU lyophilized powder for reconstitution

dosing_bands.duration_note: Loading: 2000 IU/kg IV over 30 min; Maintenance: 1000 IU/kg/hr for 24-72 hours; monitor fibrinogen, PT, aPTT every 6 hours
dosing_bands.notes: PICU/specialist use only. Type and cross-match blood before starting. Have fresh frozen plasma and cryoprecipitate available. Stop if fibrinogen <100 mg/dL.

dosing_bands.duration_note: Instil 5,000-25,000 IU in sufficient volume to fill catheter lumen; leave for 2-4 hours then aspirate; may repeat once
dosing_bands.notes: Local instillation for catheter clearance — lower systemic risk than IV thrombolysis

hepatic_note: Increased bleeding risk in hepatic impairment due to coagulopathy; use with extreme caution
contraindications: Recent (within 10 days) major surgery, trauma, or childbirth
Intracranial or intraspinal surgery within 2 months
Recent streptococcal infection (antibodies may neutralize; use alteplase instead)
cross_reactions: Prior streptokinase use (neutralizing antibodies persist 6-12 months)

interactions.effect: Markedly increased bleeding risk; hold heparin before and during infusion; restart 4-6 hours after
Increased bleeding risk; but aspirin often given concomitantly in MI protocols
Increased risk of GI bleeding

monitoring_parameters: Vital signs every 15 minutes during infusion
Signs of bleeding (all sites — IV sites, GI, intracranial)
black_box_warnings: Risk of life-threatening haemorrhage including intracranial bleeding
Anaphylaxis reported — streptococcal protein; have adrenaline available
Cannot re-use within 12 months due to neutralizing antibodies — use alteplase for repeat thrombolysis
pediatric_specific_warnings: PICU use only under haematology consultation
Neonatal use: extremely high risk of IVH — generally avoided; prefer alteplase if thrombolysis needed
Document recent streptococcal infections — anti-streptokinase antibodies reduce efficacy
Allergic reactions more common than with alteplase

administration.reconstitution: Reconstitute with 5 mL 0.9% NaCl; gently swirl — do not shake
administration.dilution: Further dilute in 50-100 mL 0.9% NaCl for infusion
administration.infusion_rate_note: Loading dose over 30 minutes; maintenance infusion via infusion pump. Monitor for hypotension and allergic reactions.
administration.compatibility_note: Compatible with 0.9% NaCl; incompatible with dextrose solutions
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C before reconstitution; use within 24 hours after reconstitution

food_instructions: Not applicable (IV use)
storage_instructions: Store lyophilized powder at 2-8°C; reconstituted solution: use within 24 hours if refrigerated
lactation_note: Unknown if excreted in breast milk; avoid during active treatment
reference_source: CHEST Guidelines on Antithrombotic Therapy in Children
ASH Guidelines on VTE in Children

unlicensed_note: Licensed for all ages including neonates; however, NOT first-choice in elective pediatric anaesthesia due to risks — reserved for emergency RSI and specific indications

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2-3 mg/kg IV (higher dose in neonates due to larger volume of distribution); onset 30-60 seconds; duration 4-6 minutes
dosing_bands.notes: Pre-treat with atropine 0.02 mg/kg (min 0.1 mg) in neonates and infants to prevent bradycardia. Higher dose required in neonates than older children.

dosing_bands.duration_note: 2 mg/kg IV; onset 30-60 seconds; duration 4-6 minutes; pre-treat with atropine 0.02 mg/kg
dosing_bands.notes: Atropine pre-treatment mandatory in infants to prevent vagal bradycardia/asystole

dosing_bands.duration_note: 1-2 mg/kg IV; onset 30-60 seconds; duration 4-6 minutes; atropine pre-treatment recommended for children <5 years
dosing_bands.notes: Reserve for emergency RSI when rocuronium not available. Ensure suction and difficult airway equipment ready.

dosing_bands.indication: Emergency intubation (IM route when IV access unavailable)
dosing_bands.duration_note: 4 mg/kg IM (deltoid or vastus lateralis); onset 2-4 minutes; use when IV access impossible in emergency
dosing_bands.notes: IM route only when IV access truly impossible; onset slower and less predictable. Must give atropine IM simultaneously.

renal_bands.adjusted_dose_note: Risk of hyperkalaemia in renal failure — AVOID if possible; use rocuronium + sugammadex instead

hepatic_note: Prolonged duration in severe liver disease due to reduced pseudocholinesterase production
contraindications: Known or family history of malignant hyperthermia
Hyperkalaemia or conditions prone to hyperkalaemia (burns >24h, crush injuries, denervation injuries, prolonged immobilisation, renal failure)
Myopathies (Duchenne, Becker) — risk of fatal rhabdomyolysis
Personal or family history of undiagnosed muscular dystrophy

interactions.effect: Anticholinesterases prolong Phase I block of succinylcholine; do not use for reversal
Prior non-depolariser may reduce onset of succinylcholine; prior succinylcholine may prolong non-depolariser
May potentiate neuromuscular blockade and increase malignant hyperthermia risk in susceptible individuals
Inhibit pseudocholinesterase — massively prolonged paralysis

monitoring_parameters: Heart rate and ECG (bradycardia, arrhythmias, hyperkalaemia)
Duration of paralysis (should recover in 5-10 minutes)
black_box_warnings: FATAL CARDIAC ARREST from hyperkalaemia reported in children with undiagnosed muscular dystrophy — NOT for routine elective paediatric anaesthesia
MALIGNANT HYPERTHERMIA: potentially fatal hypermetabolic crisis — have dantrolene available
FDA boxed warning: restrict pediatric use to emergency RSI or when immediate airway control is imperative
pediatric_specific_warnings: NOT for routine elective pediatric anaesthesia — use rocuronium instead
Undiagnosed muscular dystrophy (especially Duchenne) can cause fatal hyperkalaemic cardiac arrest
Always pre-treat infants and young children with atropine to prevent bradycardia/asystole
Bradycardia more common and more severe in children than adults, especially with repeat doses
Masseter muscle rigidity after succinylcholine may be early sign of malignant hyperthermia

administration.dilution: May dilute in 0.9% NaCl or D5W for infusion if needed
administration.infusion_rate_note: Give as rapid IV bolus over 10-30 seconds
administration.compatibility_note: Do not mix with thiopental or alkaline solutions in same syringe — precipitation
administration.storage: Store at 2-8°C; may store at room temperature for up to 14 days

administration.infusion_rate_note: 4 mg/kg IM in deltoid or vastus lateralis; onset 2-4 minutes

food_instructions: Not applicable (emergency injectable); standard pre-anaesthetic fasting if applicable
storage_instructions: Store at 2-8°C; may be stored at room temperature (up to 25°C) for max 14 days; do not use if not clear
lactation_note: Ultra-short acting; negligible transfer to breast milk after single dose
